title: Implementation details — Install addons
text:
Kubeadm installs the internal DNS server and the kube-proxy addon components via the API server.
A ServiceAccount for
kube-proxy
is created in the
kube-system
namespace; then kube-proxy is
deployed as a DaemonSet:
The credentials (
ca.crt
and
token
) to the control plane come from the ServiceAccount
The location (URL) of the API server comes from a ConfigMap
The
kube-proxy
ServiceAccount is bound to the privileges in the
system:node-proxier
ClusterRole
The CoreDNS service is named
kube-dns
for compatibility reasons with the legacy
kube-dns
addon.
A ServiceAccount for CoreDNS is created in the
kube-system
namespace.
The
coredns
ServiceAccount is bound to the privileges in the
system:coredns
ClusterRole
The CoreDNS service is named
kube-dns
for compatibility reasons with the legacy
kube-dns
addon.
A ServiceAccount for CoreDNS is created in the
kube-system
namespace.
The
coredns
ServiceAccount is bound to the privileges in the
system:coredns
ClusterRole
In Kubernetes version 1.21, support for using
kube-dns
with kubeadm was removed.
You can use CoreDNS with kubeadm even when the related Service is named
kube-dns
.
